title: Executive Order 7392—Revocation of Executive Order No. 6014 of February 6, 1933, Withdrawing Public Lands in Oregon

flags.justification: A single one-time revocation of a prior land withdrawal under existing statutory authority; no ongoing decision-making authority or position is created or exercised. Structurally identical in form to EO 7344.
No implementing actor or ongoing program exists; the order takes effect automatically 'upon the date of the official filing of the plat of resurvey.'
Single narrow purpose: revoke one specific prior land withdrawal.
A land-status revocation has no enforcement dimension.
No incentive structure is created by a status-revocation action.
Not a grant of ongoing authority; self-executing upon a defined future event, not an authority requiring review.
No existing oversight mechanism is touched.
No regulated party or compliance scheme exists.
Restores the specified township to its prior public-domain status pending a routine resurvey; no broader effects identifiable from the text.
No second agency or mandate is implicated.
No exemptions, waivers, or carve-outs are created.

notes: Validation batch, drawn negative (random sample, seed 20260703, pre-registration v1.1). Near-boilerplate land-withdrawal revocation — floor-score exemplar, nearly identical in structure to EO 7344 (different location/date only). First-pass coding; not yet cross-coded for inter-coder reliability.
